stem: Consider the system \( ([[a1]])x + ([[b1]])y = [[c1]] \) and \( ([[a2]])x + ([[b2]])y = [[c2]] \). What is the value of \( x \)?
solution: The system has the unique solution \( x = [[p]] \), \( y = [[q]] \) (its determinant is nonzero), so \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the value of \( y \), not \( x \).
This has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign for \( x \).
This adds the two unknowns instead of reporting \( x \) alone.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( x \).
This is off by one from the correct value of \( x \).
This is off by two from the correct value of \( x \).

stem: A theater sells adult tickets for \( p \) dollars and child tickets for \( q \) dollars. On one night, \( [[a1]] \) adult tickets and \( [[b1]] \) child tickets brought in \( [[c1]] \) dollars. On another night, \( [[a2]] \) adult tickets and \( [[b2]] \) child tickets brought in \( [[c2]] \) dollars. What is the price \( p \) of an adult ticket, in dollars?
solution: Solving the two revenue equations simultaneously gives \( p = [[p]] \) and \( q = [[q]] \), so the adult ticket price is \( p = [[answer]] \) dollars.
distractor_rationales: This is the price of a child ticket, not an adult ticket.
This lumps both nights' data together instead of solving the system properly.
This ignores the child tickets entirely when isolating the adult price from just one night.
This is off by one from the correct price.
This is off by one from the correct price.
This is off by two from the correct price.

stem: What is the \( x \)-coordinate of the point where the lines \( y = ([[m1]])x + ([[b1]]) \) and \( y = ([[m2]])x + ([[b2]]) \) intersect?
solution: At the intersection, both expressions for \( y \) are equal: \( ([[m1]])x + ([[b1]]) = ([[m2]])x + ([[b2]]) \). Solving for \( x \) gives \( x = \dfrac{([[b2]]) - ([[b1]])}{([[m1]]) - ([[m2]])} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the slopes in the denominator instead of subtracting them.
This has the numerator's subtraction reversed relative to the correct sign convention.
This reports the difference of the slopes instead of solving for the intersection.
This is off by one from the correct \( x \)-coordinate.
This is off by one from the correct \( x \)-coordinate.
This is off by two from the correct \( x \)-coordinate.

solution: This 3-by-3 system has the unique solution \( x = [[p]] \), \( y = [[q]] \), \( z = [[r]] \) (its coefficient determinant is nonzero), so \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the value of \( y \), not \( x \).
This is the value of \( z \), not \( x \).
This mixes up two of the other unknowns instead of reporting \( x \).
This is off by one from the correct value of \( x \).
